title: keep-id rootless container creates directory with incorrect ownership when mounting volume inside a volume at a yet-non-existing path
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Rootless podman containers with userns keep-id create directories with incorrect owner and group (uid/gid from subgid/subuid) when a volume (`--volume`) is mounted inside another volume (`--volume`) at a location which does not exist yet (non-existing intermediate directory).

Essentially:
Let `homedir` a directory on host mounted on `homedir` inside the container. Let `cachedir` a directory on host mounted on `homedir/builddir/cachedir` inside the container.

`builddir` will have the wrong owner and group.

This applies to both the host and the container. This means this creates a directory the user who started the container cannot remove or write to from the host. This also means the user inside the container cannot write to this directory either.

My use case is to pre-populate a cache directory (which needs to be RW too so it can be "autofed"!) inside a build directory which does not exist yet (created by the build system).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `mkdir homedir cachedir`

2. `podman run -it --rm --userns keep-id -v $PWD/homedir:$PWD/homedir:Z -v $PWD/cachedir:$PWD/homedir/builddir/cachedir:Z -w $PWD/homedir debian:bullseye bash`

**Describe the results you received:**

In container:
```
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ pwd
/home/qschulz/work/test/homedir
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ ls -l
total 0
drwxr-xr-t. 3 root    root    22 Aug 17 15:35 builddir
-rw-r--r--. 1 qschulz qschulz  0 Aug 17 15:35 test
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ ls -l builddir/
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 qschulz qschulz 18 Aug 17 15:35 cachedir
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ touch test
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ cd builddir/
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~/builddir$ touch test
touch: cannot touch 'test': Permission denied
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~/builddir$ cd cachedir/
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~/builddir/cachedir$ touch test
```

On host:
```
$ tree -u -g $PWD
[qschulz  qschulz ]  /home/qschulz/work/test
├── [qschulz  qschulz ]  cachedir
│   └── [qschulz  qschulz ]  test
└── [qschulz  qschulz ]  homedir
    ├── [100000   100000  ]  builddir
    │   └── [100000   100000  ]  cachedir
    └── [qschulz  qschulz ]  test

4 directories, 2 files
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
In container:
```
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ pwd
/home/qschulz/work/test/homedir
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ ls -l
total 0
drwxr-xr-t. 3 qschulz qschulz 22 Aug 17 15:35 builddir
-rw-r--r--. 1 qschulz qschulz  0 Aug 17 15:35 test
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ ls -l builddir/
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 qschulz qschulz 18 Aug 17 15:35 cachedir
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ touch test
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~$ cd builddir/
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~/builddir$ touch test
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~/builddir$ cd cachedir/
qschulz@e901ea733a98:~/builddir/cachedir$ touch test
```

On host:
```
$ tree -u -g $PWD
[qschulz  qschulz ]  /home/qschulz/work/test
├── [qschulz  qschulz ]  cachedir
│   └── [qschulz  qschulz ]  test
└── [qschulz  qschulz ]  homedir
    ├── [qschulz  qschulz ]  builddir
    │   ├── [qschulz  qschulz ]  cachedir
    │   └── [qschulz  qschulz ]  test
    └── [qschulz  qschulz ]  test

4 directories, 2 files
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

```
$ cat /etc/subuid
qschulz:100000:65536
$ cat /etc/subgid
qschulz:100000:65536
$ id -u
1000
$ id -g
1000
$ whoami
qschulz
```

The first subid/subgid is the id of the owner and group of the newly created directory on the host.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Fri Jul 22 21:05:59 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-4.1.1-3.fc36.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Not checked on latest version of podman.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 36 Server

comments.body:
I think it is the OCI runtime to create the missing parent directories.

The behavior is not specified in the OCI specs, so a runtime could create these directories either owned by root (as it happens in this case) or by the user that runs inside the container.  We could change this behavior inside crun, but it would just be an arbitrary choice, similarly to what is done now.

The safest if you care about the missing directories ownership is to create these directories in the volume before you run the container with the ownership you want or fix it later on when the container terminated with `podman unshare chown -R 0:0 $homedir $cachedir`.

I am closing it as I think it works as expected, but please reopen if you disagree
This makes a Jenkins pipeline job using containers a bit less easy to create and maintain but so be it :) Thanks for the speedy answer and suggestions.
